evals.name: user-named-substantial-pr
evals.prompt:
Please review this PR for me: https://github.com/sycamore-labs/kernel/pull/3591

I'm a maintainer-adjacent reviewer and I want a second opinion before approving. Bias toward approval unless there's a real blocking correctness issue. List optional improvements separately.
evals.expected_output: Approval-biased verdict (APPROVE or APPROVE-with-suggestions, or REQUEST CHANGES with concrete blocking finding). Markdown summary with a verdict line, optional Blocking issues section (only if any), Optional improvements section (only if any), and a 'What I looked at' note. Should ask before posting to GitHub.

evals.name: small-bugfix-pr
evals.prompt: Take a look at https://github.com/sycamore-labs/kernel/pull/3582 and let me know if it's good to ship.
evals.expected_output: For a tiny background-color bugfix, the verdict should be APPROVE (clean) or APPROVE-with-suggestions at most. No spurious blocking issues. Fast: a 6-line diff doesn't need a deep review.

evals.name: small-feature-pr
evals.prompt: Can you do a code review on https://github.com/sycamore-labs/kernel/pull/3588 ? I want to know if there's anything blocking.
evals.expected_output: Small (39/11 lines) session_id-passing fix. Default expectation: APPROVE or APPROVE-with-suggestions. Reviewer should resist the urge to invent blocking issues for a clearly-scoped fix.
